recommendations: define and run a minimal MCP smoke test suite (server start + one call each for `sample/param/svi/mcmc/nuts/trace`) in CI
add strict request/response schemas with argument validation and clear error mapping for all exposed endpoints
add version pinning and compatibility checks between `pyro`
`torch`
and plugin deps (`fastmcp`
`pydantic`) to prevent runtime drift
introduce endpoint-level timeouts/cancellation and resource guards (especially for `mcmc/nuts/predictive`) to avoid runaway jobs
add structured logging + tracing (request id
endpoint
latency
failure type) and basic metrics export
implement authentication/authorization (or at least token gating) before exposing model-execution endpoints
generate and publish an endpoint capability matrix in `README_MCP.md` with examples and parameter constraints
add contract tests to ensure MCP endpoint behavior stays aligned with upstream Pyro API signatures across upgrades
improve packaging consistency by consolidating build metadata (prefer `pyproject.toml` as source of truth) and documenting install extras
add resilience for optional dependencies (graceful degradation when `funsor/horovod/scipy/pandas` are missing)
create golden-path examples for common workflows (SVI training
NUTS sampling
posterior predictive) as executable integration tests
add reproducibility controls (seed propagation policy and deterministic-mode documentation) across plugin endpoints
strengthen security by sandboxing or limiting arbitrary model/module loading pathways
add backward-compatible naming/alias policy (e.g.
`WeighedPredictive` typo-risk) with deprecation warnings
include performance benchmarks for high-cost endpoints and regression thresholds in CI
document operational runbooks (startup checks
health probes
failure recovery) for MCP deployment

success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-mode adapter was feasible (import_feasibility ~0.9), enabling direct mapping of Pyro APIs to MCP endpoints.
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio, indicating baseline runtime operability.
Repository structure and core modules were correctly identified despite DeepWiki failure (fallback analysis succeeded).
No hard execution errors or warnings were emitted, reducing integration friction.
failure_reasons: Quality-gate gaps: original project tests were not executed/passing in this run (passed=false with empty details), so functional equivalence is unproven.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external architectural context and increasing reliance on local/static analysis only.
Telemetry fields are mostly zero/unknown (LLM calls, performance, environment), limiting confidence in performance and reproducibility claims.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully via zip fallback; appears efficient for a large repo, but per-node timing not provided.
node_performance.analysis_time: Successful structural/dependency/API analysis over a complex codebase; likely dominant phase, exact timing unavailable.
node_performance.generation_time: Code generation succeeded and produced expected MCP scaffold files and endpoint mappings; timing not isolated.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health check passed; however, no substantive endpoint contract/integration tests were recorded, so test depth is low.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Not measurable from provided data (reported 0 MB).
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate for generated scaffold (few files), but generated size/LOC fields are zero, indicating missing instrumentation.
